chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 811 - وَ كَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ: فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَجْعَلُ فِي جُبَّتِهِ بَدَلَ اَلْقُطْنِ قَزّاً هَلْ يُصَلِّي فِيهِ فَكَتَبَ «نَعَمْ لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ."

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.811 - He (Ibrahim ibn Mahziyar) wrote to Imam Abu Muhammad al-Hasan (as) asking about a man who replaces cotton in his cloak with qazz (a type of silk):
"Can he pray in it?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, there is no problem with it."
